findings.description: The PDF uses a clear multi-column tabular layout with distinct 'ESTIMATED RESOURCES' and 'ESTIMATED PERCENT' column groupings separated by visual spacing. The HTML renders this as a flat table without the grouped header structure.
findings.evidence: PDF has a two-tier header with 'ESTIMATED RESOURCES' spanning Total/Federal/State/County and 'ESTIMATED PERCENT' spanning Federal/State/County. HTML flattens these into individual column headers without the spanning group headers.

findings.description: The HTML table lacks the visual separation between the ESTIMATED RESOURCES columns and ESTIMATED PERCENT columns that exists in the PDF (vertical line/gap separator).
findings.evidence: PDF shows a clear visual divider between County (resources) and Federal (percent) columns. HTML renders all columns uniformly without this separation.

findings.description: Section headers like 'A. Division of Social Services', '1. Public Assistance Programs', and 'Total Cost For' are rendered with less visual distinction in the HTML compared to the bold/indented hierarchy in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF uses indentation levels and bold formatting to distinguish section headers, sub-headers, and line items. HTML preserves bold but loses the hierarchical indentation structure.

findings.description: Same layout issue as page 1 - the two-group column header structure is not preserved in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF 'ESTIMATED RESOURCES' and 'ESTIMATED PERCENT' grouped headers are flattened in HTML.

findings.description: The hierarchical indentation of programs under divisions (e.g., items under 'A. Division of Social Services' vs 'B. Division of Aging and Adult Services') is less clear in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows clear indentation levels for division headers vs program line items. HTML renders them with less visual hierarchy.

findings.description: Same layout issue as previous pages - grouped column headers not preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF 'ESTIMATED RESOURCES' and 'ESTIMATED PERCENT' grouped headers are flattened in HTML.

findings.description: The 'Grand Total All Programs and Administration' row formatting is less visually prominent in the HTML compared to the bold/separated styling in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows this as a clearly separated, bold summary row. HTML shows it but with less visual emphasis.

findings.description: The heading hierarchy is flat. The document uses h1 for the county name, but section headings like '1. Public Assistance Programs', '2. Public Assistance Administration', '3. Services Programs', and division sub-headings (A, B, C, D, E) are not rendered as proper heading elements (h2, h3, etc.) but are instead embedded as table row content.
findings.evidence: Sections like '1. Public Assistance Programs', 'A. Division of Social Services', '2. Public Assistance Administration', '3. Services Programs' appear only as table rows rather than structured headings. This is acceptable given the tabular nature of the document but results in a flat heading hierarchy with only one h1.

findings.description: The 'Grand Total' and 'All Programs and Administration' are split across two separate table rows in the HTML, whereas in the PDF they appear as a single combined row.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Grand Total All Programs and Administration' as one line item with values. HTML splits it into an empty row for 'Grand Total' and a data row for 'All Programs and Administration'. The data values are correct (25,379,409 / 22,186,781 / 612,992 / 2,579,637 / 87.42% / 2.42% / 10.16%).
